ddls:
CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each business process model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the business process model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the business process model */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the model */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the model was created */,
  "last_modified_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the model was last modified */,
  "version_control_enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates if version control is enabled for the model */,
  "model_properties" TEXT /* JSON column to store additional properties of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version of a business process model */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the business process model */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made in this version */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created this version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when this version was created */,
  "last_modified_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when this version was last modified */,
  "version_properties" TEXT /* JSON column to store additional properties of the version */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., draft, published, archived) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_versions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_element_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model element type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the model element type (e.g., BPMNElement, Actor) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model element type */,
  "type_properties" TEXT /* JSON column to store additional properties of the element type */,
  "type_category" TEXT /* Category of the element type (e.g., flow, event, gateway) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., modeler, analyst, admin) */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON column to store user permissions */,
  "last_login_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_element_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "element_id" TEXT /* ID of the model element */,
  "related_element_id" TEXT /* ID of the related model element */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., sequence flow, association) */,
  "relationship_properties" TEXT /* JSON column to store additional properties of the relationship */,
  "relationship_status" TEXT /* Status of the relationship (e.g., active, deleted) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id")
)
